narrative: OMX30 sits in structurally bullish positioning—8.1% above 200-MA, MACD above signal, ADX 19.6 with bullish DI bias—but faces headwinds from deteriorating global risk sentiment. Overnight declines across US equities (-0.74% to -1.21%), Asia weakness (Nikkei -1.36%, Hang Seng -1.48%), and the negative correlation signal (-0.417%) suggest caution despite domestic technicals. Rising US 10Y yields (4.491%, +0.81%) create a mixed picture: tailwinds for Swedish banks (NIM expansion) offset by headwinds for tech and real estate. The uptick in VIX (16.48) and flight-to-safety in gold suggest risk-off undercurrents that may cap upside. RSI 63.99 and Stochastic readings (K=66, D=68.2) remain neutral, offering no mean-reversion extremes.
morningBrief: Open cautiously. OMX30 technicals remain constructive—above all key moving averages with positive MACD—but global market weakness and negative correlation signal warrant measured positioning. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A, Swedbank A) are positioned for gains as US yields stay elevated, widening NIM prospects; watch for any softening in rate expectations. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) face minor headwinds from oil decline (-1.49%) but benefit from strong USD/SEK (+0.28%), supporting export pricing. Tech (Ericsson B, Hexagon B) remains under pressure from NASDAQ weakness and yield headwind. Boliden is a bright spot—gold strength (+1.24%) and firm copper (unc.) offer direct revenue support. Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET; a sharp surprise could reignite volatility. Support at 3085 (Bollinger lower), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Avoid aggressive longs until correlation signal stabilizes; lean into defensive rotations (pharma, banks).
drivers: Negative global correlation signal (-0.417%) offsetting OMX30 technical strength
US yields rising (+0.81%) pressuring tech/DCF-sensitive names, supporting banks
VIX uptick (+2.62%) and Asia weakness indicating risk-off sentiment despite OMX bull structure
Commodity divergence: gold +1.24% (defensive) vs oil -1.49% (demand concerns)

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield surge to 4.491% (+0.81%) and EUR/SEK strength support NIM expansion and FX flows into Swedish banking sector.
Oil decline (-1.49%) slightly reduces input cost concerns, but Brent weakness signals softening demand. USD/SEK strength (+0.28%) provides modest export pricing support.
NASDAQ weakness overnight (-0.89%), rising US yields compressing DCF valuations, and negative global correlation signal weigh on sentiment.
Gold +1.24% and copper +0.23% provide direct revenue tailwinds. Risk-off sentiment (VIX +2.62%, Hang Seng -1.48%) may boost gold further as safe haven.
US 10Y yield rise (+0.81%) increases refinancing costs and caps property valuations; rate-sensitive sector pressure expected.
Risk-off global sentiment, VIX uptick, and negative correlation signal trigger defensive rotation into non-cyclical names.
Oil decline signals demand softness; weak EUR/SEK (-0.29% though EUR up vs USD) slightly increases H&M's sourcing costs; risk-off mood pressures discretionary.

watchList.reason: Gold +1.24% and copper +0.23% provide direct tailwinds; defensive bid in gold (VIX +2.62%) may extend gains. Strongest fundamental setup in OMX30 today.
watchList.catalyst: Commodities strength, risk-off safe haven flows

watchList.reason: US 10Y +81bp supports NIM; EUR/SEK +0.29% boosts FX flows. Banks outperform on rising rate regimes; watch for any hawkish signals from Fed.
NASDAQ -0.89% overnight, global tech weakness, and yield headwinds converge. Avoid until sentiment stabilizes or VIX rolls over.
USD/SEK +0.28% is tailwind, but industrial weakness signals and negative global correlation create uncertainty. Wait for clearer trade flows.
Pharma defensive rotation likely in risk-off environment; flight-to-safety bid supports. Monitor for relative strength vs broader equity selloff.

keyLevels.technicalNote: OMX30 +8.1% vs 200-MA is strong; %B=0.66 (upper half Bollinger) suggests room to mean reversion on negative catalyst.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key risk catalyst—weak claims could soften rate expectations and boost equities; strong claims could reignite recession fears and VIX spike.
topRisk: Negative global correlation signal (-0.417%) and Asia weakness (Hang Seng -1.48%, Nikkei -1.36%) may override OMX30's technical strength; if VIX sustains above 17, expect capitulation in growth/tech names. Trade tension headlines (SpaceX IPO context) could accelerate risk-off.
technicalSummary: Bull market regime confirmed: +8.1% above 200-MA, +2.1% above 50-MA
MACD above signal with +0.463 histogram (bullish momentum)
RSI 63.99 and Stochastic (K=66, D=68.2) neutral—no mean reversion extremes yet
ADX 19.6 (moderate, just above 18 threshold) with bullish DI bias—moderate conviction
Bollinger %B=0.66 upper half: room for pullback toward mean on negative catalyst
52W positioning: 89% of range, -2.7% from high (no fresh breakout energy)
